REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER KOBRIN, NOEL VAN HARMONSON'S MOTION FOR ORDER PERMITTING DISCOVERY OF FINANCIAL CONDITION OF DEFENDANT JASON CHOW IS DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT THEY WILL PREVAIL ON A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, WHICH IS NOT ASSERTED IN THE OPERATIVE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT MADE IN THE UNFILED REPLY THAT THIS MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED BASED ON NOT YET PROPERLY MADE REQUEST FOR LEAVE TO AMEND UNDER CCP 473 IS NOT SUPPORTED BY ANY AUTHORITY. PLAINTIFFS' DECEMBER 9, 2016 COVER LETTER DOES NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF GOOD FAITH "MEET AND CONFER." DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS PER CCP 2023.010(C)IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,000 IS GRANTED TO BE PAID WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To: 1) Compel The Continued Deposition Of Richard Raskin; 2) Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose; And 3) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-03-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Josephine L. Alioto (415-553-6055) for Plaintiff/Moving party Jennifer Korbin.
Stephen Fleischer-Ihn (510-254-6763) for Defendant/opposing party Richard Raskin.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: compliance. Plaintiff's counsel indicates she has electronic proof of timeliness however there was an issue with e-filing vendor. Plaintiff's counsel requests for a continuance. Defense counsel has no authority to stipulate. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER KOBRIN and NOEL VAN HARMONSON's Motion To: 1) Compel The Continued Deposition Of Richard Raskin; 2) Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose; And 3) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions is OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B and Court's February 24, 2017. No courtesy copies of the motion provided. Additionally, this motion was filed on February 27, 2017 in violation of CCP 1005 and Court's February 24, 2017 order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Not Reported (D501)

Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion To: 1) Compel The Continued Deposition Of Richard Raskin; 2) Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose; And 3) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions

MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Josephine L. Alioto (415-553-6055) for Plaintiff/Moving party Jennifer Korbin.
Stephen Fleischer-Ihn (510-254-6763) for Defendant/opposing party Richard Raskin.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: compliance. Plaintiff's counsel indicates she has electronic proof of timeliness however there was an issue with e-filing vendor. Plaintiff's counsel requests for a continuance. Defense counsel has no authority to stipulate. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFFS JENNIFER KOBRIN and NOEL VAN HARMONSON's Motion To: 1) Compel The Continued Deposition Of Richard Raskin; 2) Reopen Discovery For Limited Purpose; And 3) For The Award Of Monetary Sanctions is OFF CALENDAR. Motion And Notice Of Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Condition Of Defendant Jason Chow
Off Calendar Jan-25-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Eric Lifschitz, Esq. (415-553-6055) for Plaintiff/Moving party JENNIFER KOBRIN, NOEL VAN HARMONSON.
John Toal, Esq, (415-438-6690) for Defendant/opposing party Jason Chow.

The Court announces the tentaive ruling and that this is not proper before the Court. ARGUED. Plaintiff argues stipulation to remove punitive in complaint, relieve burden on trial ,judicial economy and admists mistake. Defendant argues stipulation, meet and confer, facts re punitive damages. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF JENNIFER KOBRIN, NOEL VAN HARMONSON's Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of Financial Condition Of Defendant Jason Chow is DENIED. Plaintiffs failed to establish that they will prevail on a claim for punitive damages, which is not asserted in the operative First Amended Complaint. Plaintiff's argument made in the unfiled reply that this motion should be granted based on not yet properly made request for leave to amend under CCP 473 is not supported by any authority. Plaintiffs' December 9, 2016 cover letter does not meet the requirements of good faith "meet and confer." Defendant's request for sanctions per CCP 2023.010(c)in the amount of $2,000 is granted to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order. Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)
